description: Controls module power states and local resets via MDCTL/PTCMD/PTSTAT/MDSTAT; must be sequenced before any peripheral access; used for deterministic bring-up and runtime gating.
dependencies: Main PLL; PSC clock; Target module clocks

description: Generates system clocks with programmable PLLM/PLLDIVn and GO/lock handshake; must switch out of BYPASS only after lock; stable clocks are prerequisites for DDR and buses.
dependencies: Reference clock; PSC for downstream modules

description: Selects alternate functions for multiplexed pins to route McBSP/EMIFA/PCI/I2C/SPI/UART/GPIO; configure after PSC enable and before peripheral init.
Maps peripheral/EDMA events to CPU INT4–INT15 and works with CPU IER/IFR; prevents overlaps and prioritizes latency‑critical ISRs.
Aggregates many low‑priority events onto shared INT lines using EVTMASK/EVTFLAG/EVTCLR; service multiple sources in one ISR.
Programs PaRAM sets and controls events/interrupts for memory/peripheral transfers; supports linking/chaining and completion via TCC.
Executes scheduled transfers with tunable queue priorities/watermarks; monitor TCSTATUS for starvation and balance channel‑to‑queue mapping.
CPU‑triggered transfers via PaRAM trigger word for low‑latency copies; manage CIPR/CIER/CICR and guard against active PaRAM writes.
Detects missed events and faults via EMR/CCERR; clear and recover channels; persistent errors often indicate address/permission issues.
Initializes external SDRAM with SDCR/SDTIMR1/2/SDRCR and PHY/VTP; only after stable PLL/PSC; verify with memory test.
Provides async access to NOR/NAND/SRAM with per‑CS timing (A1CR..A4CR
dependencies: NANDFCR); correct mux/timing prevents contention and data errors.

description: Implements 32‑bit PCI master/target; program local config (BARs
dependencies: command/status) and address windows; integrate EDMA for throughput.

description: Parallel host access to DSP memory via HPIA/HPID/HPIC; configure ownership/endianness; useful for boot/diagnostics.
TDM/framed serial with SRGR/PCR/SPCR/RCR/XCR; supports EDMA and strict reset/enable ordering.
Full‑duplex serial with SPIGCR/SPIFMT and SPIDAT/SPIBUF; handle CPOL/CPHA and chip‑select timing; EDMA optional.
16550‑style with baud via DLL/DLH and frame via LCR; use FIFOs/IIR; requires accurate input clock.
Master/slave with ICPSC/ICCLKL/H and ICMDR; implement timeouts/NACK/bus‑clear for robustness.
Up to 32 pins with DIR01
dependencies: SET/CLR_DATA and edge/latched status via INSTAT01; banked IRQs via BINTEN.

description: Periodic or capture timing via TGCR/TCR and PRD/TIM; map IRQs with INTMUX; ensure clock accuracy and ISR budget.
Control cacheability via MARx and L1/L2 cache policy; align with DMA placement or add maintenance to avoid coherency bugs.
ROM selects boot mode via straps and CFG; firmware should sanitize PLL/PSC and log boot status before proceeding.
Read‑only IDs enable errata gating and capability detection; log at boot for fleet diagnostics.
